successCriteria.description: src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts exists and exports AuditRecord with fields id, entityType, entityId, and action, and exports CreateAuditRecordInput with entityType, entityId, and action.

src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts exists and exports AuditRepository with create(input) and findByEntity(entityType, entityId) method signatures using AuditRecord and CreateAuditRecordInput from audit.model.ts.
src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts exports an abstract PostgreSqlAuditRepository class implementing the AuditRepository contract with matching abstract method signatures.
Repository contracts depend on AuditRecord types imported from src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts and introduce no circular dependencies.
The canonical audit_records schema is represented exactly as specified: id UUID PRIMARY KEY, entity_type VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL, entity_id UUID NOT NULL, action VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL.
tests/unit/modules/audit/audit.repository.spec.ts exists, uses Vitest, and verifies audit module contract exports and TypeScript type compatibility.

constraints: Scope is limited to audit persistence contracts and related type definitions/tests only.
Create approximately 3-5 files within the audit module and unit test area.
Use the repository pattern: all database access must be represented through repository interfaces/contracts; do not implement direct database access.
AuditRecord and CreateAuditRecordInput definitions must match the architecture specification exactly.
PostgreSqlAuditRepository must be an abstract contract, not a concrete implementation.
All repository method signatures must use types imported from src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts.
Avoid circular dependencies between audit model and repository files.
Use TypeScript, Vitest, and existing project conventions.
Audit records are intended to be reusable by later workflow services; do not couple contracts to leave-specific logic.
Input validation at API boundaries is a project-wide principle but no API endpoints are part of this phase.
RBAC enforcement is a project-wide principle but no API endpoints are part of this phase.
No sensitive data in logs; this phase should not introduce logging of sensitive information.
Error handling principle applies to future implementations; this phase should define contracts only and must not introduce unhandled async behavior.
outOfScope: Creating database migrations or executing schema changes beyond defining the canonical schema contract.
Leave request submission, approval, rejection, or workflow services.
API routes, controllers, request validation, or RBAC enforcement endpoints.
Employee domain models or repository contracts.
LeaveBalance or LeavePolicy domain models or repositories.
Notification domain models, repositories, or services.
Audit service layer logic or workflow integration.
Any changes outside the audit module contracts and the specified unit test file.
